---
title: "On Wednesdays We Write in Pink"
date: "2026-05-06"
categories:
  - Mussel Biomarkers
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false

editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

### Plan of the Week: May 4 - May 10, 2026

#### *High- level outline for the week. Adjusted daily to reflect progress of the day before*

-   This week is all about getting biomarkers out!

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<div>

> ~~Monday - Catching up & weekly planning~~
>
> ~~Tuesday - Steven Working Session & UW-RUA~~
>
> Wednesday - Biomarkers & UG Bench Prep
>
> Thursday - Biomarkers & Bench Work
>
> Friday - Biomarkers & Bench
>
> Saturday - Biomarkers
>
> Sunday - Afternoon Methylation Work

</div>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Plan of the Day

### *Granular level task list to accomplish the high- level goal outlined above*

-   My goals for today are:
    -   Wrap-up biomarkers results and discussion
    -   Clean up the biomarker manuscript folder to include in review
    -   Prep for UG bench support on Thursday

### Projects Touched Today

-   Mussel Biomarkers

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Progress Notes

-   Today started with a daily planning session, prep for the handful of
    meetings I have, and outlining the biomarker results re-work.
    -   First up was task dumping and daily prep, today's working block
        will focus on re-working the results section of the biomarker
        paper by:
        -   Reorganizing the order results are delivered, spatial first,
            followed by biomarker activity and IBR scores.
    -   Next was lab meeting where we did a check-in and supported an
        undergrad in preparing for the presentation of their work.
-   I printed the draft visuals for the biomarker paper to help me keep
    the bigger picture in mind and ensure the narrative the viz tells is
    aligned with what I write.
-   I met with folks from Puget Sound Partnership to discuss an upcoming
    conservation event.
-   Turning back to biomarkers, I drafted a high level outline of topic
    sentences that match the results to the discussion.
    -   After re-reading what I have already written, I was able to
        re-work most of what was already there, by stripping it down to
        elementary- level sentences and removing any interpretation.
    -   Next I did the same with the discussion and then the last 3
        paragraphs of the introduction. The doc looks crazy, but it
        helps me see the narrative clearer.
-   I wrapped up the day by attending the professional development
    programming postmortem with the academic affairs team.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Outcomes: Products & Word Count

-   Drafting biomarker revisions: 431 words

<div>

> **Today's total: 431 words**
>
> **Monthly total to date: 817 words**
>
> **Annual total to date: 41,979 words**
>
> **Annual target total to date: 61,000 words**

</div>

### Next Up: Tomorrow's Plan

-   Continue biomarker work and prep for getting Cole setup in the lab.
